---
title: "Week 3 — Build"
description: "Full integration, 3D printing, and user testing"
---

```{=html}
<div class="week-hero week-hero-w3">
  <div class="week-num" style="color:#FB923C">// WEEK THREE &nbsp;·&nbsp; 第三周</div>
  <div class="week-title">Build</div>
  <div class="week-zh">制造</div>
  <p class="week-desc">
    The pieces exist. Now you make them into a product.
    Week 3 is the most intense week: soldering, firmware integration, 3D printing, and relentless user testing.
    By Friday you should have a <strong>working robot in an enclosure</strong>.
  </p>
</div>
```

## Learning Objectives

By the end of Week 3 you will be able to:

- Integrate all hardware components (sensors, display, motor/speaker) into a unified system
- Print and assemble a 3D-modeled enclosure
- Run structured user tests and incorporate feedback into the product
- Use Claude Code, Codex, or other AI tools to accelerate firmware development
- Prepare a working demo for Week 4

---

## Sessions

### Session 7 — Full Hardware Integration

Everything on the breadboard; make it work together.

**Agenda:**
- Solder your circuit (move off the breadboard if components are confirmed)
- Integrate all sensors and actuators: camera, motor, speaker, display
- Firmware: handle all hardware in one ESP-Claw sketch
- Stress test: run continuously for 10 minutes — what breaks?
- Bug triage with instructors

::: {.callout-warning title="The integration wall"}
Most teams hit a wall here — something that worked separately breaks when combined. **This is normal.** Budget 50% of this session for debugging. The debugging is the learning.
:::

**Common integration issues to watch for:**

| Symptom | Likely cause |
|---|---|
| Device crashes on startup | Power rail can't supply enough current |
| Display garbled | I2C address conflict |
| LLM calls time out | Wi-Fi and heavy compute conflict |
| Motor causes resets | Motor driver needs separate power |

---

### Session 8 — 3D Print & Assemble

Your robot gets a body.

::: {.callout-tip title="Design for printability"}
The best enclosure is the one that actually prints. Design with **0.2mm layer height** in mind, avoid overhangs greater than 45°, and add clearance for USB ports and cables.
:::

**Agenda:**
- Finalize enclosure design in Fusion 360 / TinkerCAD / Blender
- Submit print jobs (prints typically take 3–8 hours)
- While printing: refine firmware, document wiring
- Assemble when print is complete: mount board, route cables, close enclosure
- Reality check: does it still feel like your concept brief?

**3D modeling tools:**

| Tool | Best for | Free? |
|---|---|---|
| TinkerCAD | Simple shapes, beginners | ✅ Yes |
| Fusion 360 | Parametric design, precise fits | ✅ Student/edu |
| Blender | Organic/sculpted shapes | ✅ Yes |
| SolidWorks | Professional mechanical design | ❌ License needed |

---

### Session 9 — User Testing & Iteration

Put the product in front of real people. Fix what matters.

::: {.callout-note title="Structured user testing"}
A good user test takes 15 minutes per person. Give them a task, watch silently, take notes. Don't explain or defend — just observe.
:::

**Agenda:**
- Each team runs 3–4 user tests (find people outside the class)
- Observe: what confuses people? What delights them? What do they ignore?
- Debrief: what are the top 3 things to fix before Week 4?
- Sprint: make the 3 fixes (if more than a day of work, scope them down)
- Final hardware check with instructor sign-off

**What to test:**
- Can a new user figure out how to interact with the robot without explanation?
- Does the robot's personality come through clearly?
- Is the physical form coherent with the personality?
- Does it work reliably for 5 minutes straight?

---

::: {.deliverables}
#### ✓ Week 3 Deliverables

- [ ] **Working robot in enclosure** — fully integrated, reliable, battery/USB powered
- [ ] **User test report** — 3–4 testers, observations, top changes made
- [ ] **Source code** — commented, committed to GitHub
- [ ] **Wiring diagram** — documented schematic
- [ ] **Demo-ready** — 2-minute rehearsed demo of the robot's key interaction
:::

---

## Resources for Week 3

- [ESP-Claw hardware integration guide](https://esp-claw.com/en/) — multi-component examples
- [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) — AI pair programmer in your terminal
- [TinkerCAD](https://www.tinkercad.com) — web-based 3D modeling (free)
- [Fusion 360 for students](https://www.autodesk.com/education) — professional-grade free license
- [Prusa Slicer](https://www.prusa3d.com/prusaslicer/) — open source slicer for most printers

---

*← [Week 2 — Design](week2.qmd) &nbsp;|&nbsp; [Week 4 — Pitch It →](week4.qmd)*
